dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.description: Real-time overview of your AetherProxy instance — system resources, active connections, and network throughput.

nodes.title: Nodes
nodes.description: Manage remote VPS nodes for multi-server deployments. AetherProxy SSH-deploys sing-box to each node and health-checks it every 30 seconds.
nodes.name: Name
nodes.phase1Note: Phase 1 shows sing-box inbounds. Multi-VPS node management arrives in Phase 2.
nodes.remoteNodesDescription: Manage remote VPS nodes. AetherProxy health-checks them every 30s.
nodes.noRemoteNodes: No remote nodes yet. Click Add Node to register a VPS.
nodes.localInboundsDescription: Local sing-box inbounds on this server.
nodes.gossipDescriptionBefore: When active, this node participates in a gossip cluster
nodes.gossipDescriptionAfter: and discovers other AetherProxy instances without a central server.
nodes.bootstrapPeersPrefix: Bootstrap peers can be configured via the
nodes.joinNetworkToStart: Join the decentralized network to start.
nodes.resetHostKeyConfirm: Clear the stored SSH host key for this node? The next connection will re-learn and pin the server's public key (Trust-On-First-Use).
nodes.resetHostKeySuccess: Host key cleared. The next deploy will re-pin the server's key.
nodes.sshKeyHelperText: To generate a key: run ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/aetherproxy_node on your server. Add the .pub file to authorized_keys on the remote node. Enter the private key path above.

users.title: Users
users.description: Manage client accounts. Each user receives auto-generated credentials for every inbound protocol and a unique subscription URL.
users.credentialsHint: Auto-generated credentials for all proxy protocols. Leave unchanged to keep existing or regenerate below.
users.qrDialogHint: Scan this QR in Hiddify, Clash, or any compatible app.

inbounds.title: Inbounds
inbounds.description: Configure sing-box inbound listeners — the ports and protocols your clients connect to. Each inbound can be bound to a TLS profile for encryption.
inbounds.confirmDelete: Delete this inbound? All associated client links will be removed.

subscriptions.title: Subscriptions
subscriptions.description: Share connection links with your users. Each client has a unique subscription URL that bundles all configured inbound links into a single importable profile.
subscriptions.clientSubsHint: Select a user to get their subscription link and QR code. Import into Hiddify, Clash, or any compatible app.
subscriptions.noClients: No users found. Create users first.
subscriptions.howToConnectStep1New: Create token below or paste an existing token.
subscriptions.howToConnectStep2: Click Generate to create the share link and QR code.
subscriptions.howToConnectStep3: Import the link or scan the QR in your client app.
subscriptions.scanHint: Scan the matching QR in your app, or copy a link above.
subscriptions.apiTokensHint: These tokens are for programmatic access to the AetherProxy API (e.g. for automation scripts). They are not proxy subscription tokens.

routing.title: Routing Rules
routing.description: Routing rules determine how sing-box forwards traffic between inbounds and outbounds. Rules are evaluated top-to-bottom — the first match wins.
routing.noRules: No routing rules. Click Add Rule to create one.
routing.inboundHint: Comma-separated inbound tags to match (e.g. vless-in, hy2-in)
routing.outboundHint: Tag of the outbound to route matched traffic to (e.g. direct, warp-out)
routing.domainKeywordHint: Comma-separated keywords found anywhere in the domain
routing.protocolHint: Comma-separated protocols: tls, quic, http, dns…
routing.ruleSetHint: Comma-separated rule-set names defined in the sing-box route.rule_set config
routing.outboundRequiredNote: ⚠ An outbound tag is required for each rule.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.description: Traffic and usage statistics across protocols, clients, and inbounds. Select a time window using the buttons above.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Configure AetherProxy panel parameters. Most settings take effect immediately after saving; core-level changes may require a Core restart.
settings.newUsername: New username (leave blank to keep current)
settings.portSyncLoadError: Failed to load port sync status.

plugins.title: Plugins
plugins.description: AetherProxy outbound plugins extend sing-box with custom obfuscation. Place compiled .so plugins in the plugins/ directory. Transport plugins (h2disguise, wscdn, grpcobfs, mux) alter the TCP transport layer — only one should be enabled at a time. The ECH plugin operates at the TLS layer and can be combined with any transport plugin.
plugins.name: Name
plugins.transportConflictWarning: Multiple transport plugins are enabled ({names}). Only one transport plugin should be active at a time — they all write to the same transport field.

outbounds.title: Outbounds
outbounds.description: Outbound proxies define how traffic exits sing-box. Add direct, block, or proxy outbounds (VLESS, Hysteria2, Trojan, etc.). Use the ⚡ Test button on each row to verify live connectivity.
outbounds.tagHint: Unique identifier referenced by routing rules (e.g. warp-out, cdn-relay)
outbounds.typeHint: Outbound type: direct, block, selector, urltest, vless, hysteria2, trojan, shadowsocks, socks, http…
outbounds.optionsJsonHint: Additional type-specific fields as JSON (e.g. server, port, uuid, password, tls settings). Leave {} for simple types.

endpoints.title: Endpoints
endpoints.description: Network-layer endpoints such as Cloudflare WARP or custom WireGuard peers that can be referenced by outbound rules.
endpoints.tagHint: Unique identifier for this endpoint (e.g. warp, wg-peer1)
endpoints.typeHint: warp for Cloudflare WARP, wireguard for a custom WireGuard peer
endpoints.optionsJsonHint: Endpoint-specific fields: local_address, private_key, peer public key, endpoint address, etc.

services.title: Services
services.description: Supplementary sing-box service objects (e.g. DNS-over-HTTPS, custom listeners) that run alongside inbounds. Configuration uses raw JSON.
services.tagHint: Unique identifier for this service (e.g. dns-local, doh-service)
services.typeHint: sing-box service type: dns, http, mixed, socks, tun, redirect, tproxy…
services.tlsIdHint: TLS profile ID from the Inbounds TLS section. Use 0 for no TLS.
services.optionsJsonHint: Service-specific fields as JSON (address, listen_port, servers, inbounds, etc.).
